PermitPulse/ Sample Report Who it helps Red Tape Index View Sample
Permit Review Plus / Los Angeles

See what a PermitPulse report looks like before you order.

PermitPulse turns public-record permit signals into first-pass property clarity. This sample shows a one-address public-record snapshot built to reduce surprises before you buy, bid, build, list, or quote.

Send One Address View Sample Report Explore Red Tape Index

No spam. No obligation. PermitPulse is a first-pass public-record review. It is not a legal opinion, architectural opinion, title report, survey, code determination, or permit approval guarantee.

Sample Report format
What’s Inside

The report is built for a fast read.

Open it, scan the top line, then decide what needs a call, pause, confirmation, or deeper professional review.

01 / Snapshot

Decision Snapshot

The plain-English read on one property address and the next decision in front of you.

02 / Sources

Official Source Checklist

Which public permit, parcel, planning, zoning, or assessor-style sources were checked.

03 / Log

PermitPulse Research Log

Searches, source dates, portal notes, access limits, and records that still need manual pull.

04 / Evidence

Evidence Screenshots

Selected screenshots and references from official public-record sources where available.

05 / Flags

Manual Confirmation Flags

Items that should be verified with the city, owner, client, source office, or licensed professional.

06 / Move

Recommended Next Step

The next practical question, request, pause, or escalation before money moves.

Who It’s For

Built for people who need clarity before they commit.

01

ADU builders

Screen a lead before promising a path.

02

Remodel contractors

Check permit history before quoting scope.

03

Permit expeditors

Start intake with a cleaner record picture.

04

Roofers

Spot property and record issues before a rush bid.

05

Solar contractors

Check obvious public-record risk before design work.

06

Real estate agents

Help clients understand what needs verification.

07

Investors

Reduce surprises before offer, escrow, or rehab budget.

08

Property owners

See what the public record appears to show.

Why It’s Different

Most permit software helps after the process starts. PermitPulse helps before the expensive mistake.

City portals show records. PermitPulse turns those records into a plain-English risk snapshot. One address, one clear PDF.

Send One Address Order Permit Review Plus
What the Sample Shows

A redacted look at the format before you order.

The sample shows the kind of public-record evidence and plain-English notes used in a Permit Review Plus report.

  • Official-source screenshots
  • Address and APN confirmation where available
  • Permit history notes
  • Planning and zoning notes
  • Manual confirmation flags
  • Recommended next move
Decision Snapshot sample

Overall readout, checked categories, risk notes, and source context for one property.

Send One Address View Sample Report
Preview Pages

Skim the proof pages.

These redacted pages show how source notes, permit history, and official evidence are packaged for quick review.

Cover

Scope, address redaction, and the report frame.

One-page cheat sheet

Fast findings for the decision-maker who needs the short version.

Executive summary

Plain-English readout of what appears in public records.

Permit history

Permit record timeline and signals needing manual confirmation.

What to do next

Practical next checks before bidding, buying, listing, quoting, or submitting.

Official evidence

Source-log style evidence from official/public records where available.

Offer

Permit Review Plus, $149

Permit Review Plus is a paid one-address decision snapshot backed by official public records.

Standard$149
Target delivery24 to 48 business hours after address and notes are received

Express Permit Review Plus

Express Permit Review Plus is $300 when available. It is priority review for urgent decisions, with target turnaround of 4 business hours or less when scheduling and source access allow.

Before you hire an expeditor, call the city, or promise a client a timeline, run the address through PermitPulse.
